content.problemStatement:
Hrishi's daughters likes to eat laddoos a lot. \


There are **N** sets of laddoos prepared for Diwali; for each **i** (1 ≤ **i** ≤ **N**), the **i**-th set contains **Ai** laddoos.



Hrishi will come home in **H** hours and his daughters want to finish all the laddoos before he returns.



His daughters can eat **K** laddoos per hour. \
Each hour, they will choose some set of laddoos. 
- If this set contains at least **K** laddoos, then they will eat exactly **K** ladoos from it. 
- Otherwise, they eat the whole set but they wont eat from any other set during this hour.

What is the **minimum** **K** such that they are able to eat all the laddoos in **H** hours.


Input Format
The first line of the input contains a single integer **T** denoting the number of test cases. The description of **T** test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers **N** and **H** denoting the number of sets and the number of hours after which Hrishi will come home.
The second line contains **N** space-separated integers **A1, A2, ..., AN**.
Output
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum possible value of **K**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two space‑separated integers N and H. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum integer K such that the daughters can finish all laddoos within H hours.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: With K=3 each set finishes in one hour, total 3 hours.
Test case 2: K=2 gives hours = 1+1+2 = 4, which fits H=4.
Test case 3: K=4 gives hours = 1+1+1+2 = 5, exactly H.

solution.approach: For a fixed eating speed K, the number of hours needed for a set with Ai laddoos is ceil(Ai / K). Because each hour the daughters work on exactly one set, the total hours required is sum_{i=1..N} ceil(Ai / K). The condition we need is sum ceil(Ai / K) ≤ H. The function f(K) = sum ceil(Ai / K) is monotonic non‑increasing with K, therefore we can binary‑search the smallest K in the range [1, max(Ai)]. For each mid value we compute the sum, stopping early if it already exceeds H to keep the check O(N). The overall complexity per test case is O(N log maxAi).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log M) per test case, where M = max(Ai) (≤ 10^9).
